### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

Hello,

Since enabling persistent user sessions, our app _sometimes_ gets a 500 at the token step of an OIDC login.
We detect this most often in our test system during the first login of a federated user: I don't think we ever managed to reproduce it afterwards.

This only happens if the `PERSISTENT_USER_SESSIONS` feature is enabled, so we started getting this error with KC 26.0.x. I do not know if this happens with KC 25, so unsure if this is a regression.

### Version

26.0.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Successful LDAP user login

### Actual behavior

Login fails, because the protocol/openid-connect/token endpoint returns 500.
The user can login by simply running the OIDC flow again: they will immediately be logged in without the need to input their user name and password.

### How to Reproduce?

Ensure the `PERSISTENT_USER_SESSIONS` feature is enabled.
Most successful to reproduce for the first login of a federated user.
Might be easier to reproduce when the hardware specs are a bit low (i.e. 1 KC instance on a small machine), but we've seen it once on a bigger system too (with multiple KC instances and good hardware specs).

_No response_

issue_title: Roll-back change to startup timeout
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

[Roll-back the change to increase startup timeout to 1 hour;](https://github.com/stianst/keycloak/commit/cdebe8c2a384390c45282962e1eda1c3ac56faf9) this can have negative side-effects if KC fails to start in a timely manner during non-migrations.

We should not rush a fix for this issue days before doing 26.1 release; and rather come up with a proper plan for 26.2; and consider whether or not that is something that can be backported to 26.1.x or not.

This has been this way for a very long time, so there is no urgency here, and we should rather deliver a proper fix than rush things.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Hanging during startup is detected in a timely manner

### Actual behavior

Hanging during startup is not detected until 1 hour, which is way to long

### How to Reproduce?

N/A

### Anything else?

_No response_

Revert "fix: increasing the startup timeout to 1 hour" (#36358)

issue_title: "identity-provider-redirector" does not forward LOGIN_HINT of authentication session
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

Using the `identity-provider-redirector` the LOGIN_HINT on the session is not forwarded to the IDP.

Having a look at the code it seems like the redirect function is always called with the loginHint being null: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/40f39f3f0953b70f8f6d012d25cfb2980e9c1041/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/browser/IdentityProviderAuthenticator.java#L77-L79

Instead of always using null here, the value should be taken from the AuthenticationSession, maybe like this:

```java
protected void redirect(AuthenticationFlowContext context, String providerId) {
    String loginHint = context.getAuthenticationSession().getClientNote(OIDCLoginProtocol.LOGIN_HINT_PARAM);
    redirect(context, providerId, loginHint);
}
```

I stumbled upon this issue when having a look at: https://github.com/sventorben/keycloak-home-idp-discovery/discussions/396#discussioncomment-10066716 

### Version

25.0.5 (but latest should also be affected)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If there is a LOGIN_HINT set on the LoginSession this should be forwarded.

### Actual behavior

None is forwarded.

### How to Reproduce?

Setup flow like mentioned here: https://github.com/sventorben/keycloak-home-idp-discovery/discussions/396#discussioncomment-10066716  

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Forward LOGIN_HINT of authentication session with identity-provider-redirector

Fixes keycloak#36396

Signed-off-by: Oliver Cremerius <antikalk@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: Metric `vendor_jgroups_*` is unstable and can change in upcoming releases
issue_body:
The metrics `vendor_jgroups_*` are only to be used for debugging, and not for dashboards.

They are to be replaced with new metric names in an upcoming release of Infinispan and are then planned to stable.
commit_message:
Update the docs that `vendor_jgroups_` metrics might change between releases

Closes #36401

issue_title: Verify email required action shows presents message that email was sent even on errors
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Even if the email was not sent in the verify email required action, the UI still shows "An email with instructions to verify your email address has been sent to your address {0}."

In other places, for example in the reset credential flow, an error message is shown if the email can't be sent

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Show an error message if the email wasn't sent, to the user can contact the admin.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak claims that it sent the email. The user searches their email inbox and finds nothing / waits indefinitely. 

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a user with an email address
* Add the required action verify email

### Anything else?

Showing an error message when email sending fails is also something that helps people to try out Keycloak. Once they enable verification of emails, they probably missed to setup SMTP first. So this gives them a better hint that they need to look at the SMTP configurations. 

We could go one step further to have check that enabling a required action would check that email is configured for the realm - this could be a follow-up task.

I'll prepare a PR.
commit_message:
Show error when sending the validation email fails

Closes #36409

Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

issue_title: DPoP: Refresh token created with DPoP can be refreshed without proof
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

* When I enable the DPoP feature, I can create a DPoP refresh token on any client, even if I don't enable "OAuth 2.0 DPoP Bound Access Tokens Enabled" for that client. 
* For a DPoP bound refresh token, I can then refresh it without DPoP proof for a non-DPoP bound refresh and access token as long as "OAuth 2.0 DPoP Bound Access Tokens Enabled" is not enabled, even though the spec says both the access and the refresh token should be bound to the DPoP key. 

From a security point of view, this might be surprising: Assuminging someone enabled the DPoP feature, and didn't switch on the flag on the client level and then issued themselves a DPoP and a refresh token created with DPoP. Now they can still use the refresh token without a DPoP proof, which is against the spec. 


### Version

26.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

* Allow clients to opt in to use DPoP if the option is off, as it allows experimenting with DPoP and gradually rolling out DPoP
* Option in the client config should be named "Require Demonstrating Proof of Possession (DPoP) header in token requests" (similar to Okta's configuation in https://developer.okta.com/docs/guides/dpop/nonoktaresourceserver/main/) or "Only issue DPoP bound access and refresh tokens" (which would take the perspective of Keycloak, and the help could reference the DPoP spec and the header requirements) 
* If a refresh token has been issued with DPoP, the next refresh needs to use enforce DPoP

### Actual behavior

Already works as expected:
* Allow clients to opt in to use DPoP if the option is off, as it allows experimenting with DPoP and gradually rolling out DPoP

Actual behavior (not yet as expected): 
* Option in the client config is named "OAuth 2.0 DPoP Bound Access Tokens Enabled"
* If a refresh token has been issued with DPoP, the next refresh DOES NOT enforce DPoP, and the generated access tokens and refresh tokens are not bound to DPoP

### How to Reproduce?

* Start Keycloak with the DPoP feature enabled
* Create a public client that is not enforcing DPoP 
* Use a DPoP client (like https://github.com/panva/openid-client) to create a DPoP bound access and refresh token
* Then refresh the token without a DPoP headers -> this works, and the access and refresh tokens issued can be used without a DPoP header



### Anything else?

As discussed on the Keycloak security mailing list, this is considered a hardening issue. 

Comment from @mposolda:

> - Agree with your suggestion to rename the client option to something like "Enforce/require" instead of "Enable" . IMO we can rename the labels in the UI and java methods on OIDCAdvancedConfigWrapper, but still keep the name of the client attribute (to avoid issues with migration/backwards compatibility)
> - IMO when the DPoP feature is enabled, we can still allow clients without enforcing DPoP (so with the switch above OFF) to issue DPoP refresh tokens and access tokens. In that case, when DPoP tokens were used, it can be ideal if we also enforce DPoP at token verification. We can take a look at the claims in the refresh token (or access token) and if DPoP claims are present, we should verify DPoP. This will allow clients to migrate/test DPoP without enforcing it, which is possibly aligned with your suggestion? It can be also good to doublecheck the specification in this regards. I am just not 100% sure if we can reliably detect DPoP used by claims in the tokens as standard claims like "cnf" are not DPoP specific. For access tokens, we probably can due the token type "dpop", but not sure about refresh tokens. Maybe if needed, we can add some custom claim into tokens to detect that it is DPoP refresh token, not sure....

Comment from me: 

* For the access tokens, we already have have in the tokens "typ" set to "DPoP". I didn't find that in the standard
* The refresh tokens are AFAIK opaque, so it might be ok to use the "cnf" if we don't use it in other places.
commit_message:
DPoP: Refresh token created with DPoP can be refreshed without proof

closes #36475

Signed-off-by: Takashi Norimatsu <takashi.norimatsu.ws@hitachi.com>

issue_title: DPoP: User Info Endpoint authorization type mismatch
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When testing DPoP, I found that Keycloak's user info endpoint accepts a regular access token with both "Authorization: DPoP ..." and "Authorization: Bearer ...".
Looking at the code, this seems to be limited to the user info endpoint. 

I tested this with KC 26.0.7 and main. It works even if the DPoP feature is not enabled. 


### Version

26.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

* An "Authorization: DPoP ..." header should only work when the DPoP feature is enabled.
* An "Authorization: Bearer ..." should never accept an access token issued for DPoP.
* Keycloak should show an error when more than one "Authorization: ... " header is presented by the client. See https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9449#section-7.2 for the requirement

Implementation note: Currently we first extract, then check the token, and finally check the DPoP proof. Given the current structure of the code, maybe it might be good to move the validation around DPoP to when we extract the token from the HTTP header, so we don't lose the information about "Bearer" vs. "DPoP". 



### Actual behavior

* An "Authorization: DPoP ..." header works all the time, even if DPoP is not enabled
* An "Authorization: Bearer ..." accepts a DPoP issued token as long as it is accompanied with the DPoP proof header
* Keycloak picks only the first authorization header


### How to Reproduce?

* Start up Keycloak with DPoP disabled
* Create an access token and send it to the user info endpoint with "Authorization: DPoP ..." -> KC will accept it

### Anything else?

This has been discussed on the Keycloak security mailing list and was considered a hardening issue. 

Implementation note: Currently we first extract, then check the token, and finally check the DPoP proof. Given the current structure of the code, maybe it might be good to move the validation around DPoP to when we extract the token from the HTTP header, so we don't lose the information about "Bearer" vs. "DPoP". 

Comment from @mposolda:

> Agree with your suggestions to allow DPoP just when DPoP feature is enabled. Also to reject the request if more "Authorization" headers are used (I believe this is aligned with the specification [1]). Also +1 to your suggestion regarding "implementation note"
commit_message:
DPoP: User Info Endpoint authorization type mismatch

closes #36476

issue_title: The root cause of error is suppressed in KC 26 at building
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dependencies

### Describe the bug

If we have any error at KC's building phase, then the real root cause of  error is suppressed. Threrefore the KC's admin is not able to detect real root cause of error.

In KC 25 and less KC's admin is able to see real stacktrace. This bug is reproduced in version 26 only.

### Version

26.0.8

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The stacktrace looks like:
```
...
Caused by: java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: some.required.ClassName
  at io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader.loadClass(QuarkusClassLoader.java:525)
  at io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader.loadClass(QuarkusClassLoader.java:473)
  at io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader.loadClass(QuarkusClassLoader.java:523)
  at io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader.loadClass(QuarkusClassLoader.java:473)
  ... 24 more
```

And root cause of error is clear. We missed dependency in `providers` directory.

### Actual behavior

The stacktrace looks like:
```
ERROR: Failed to run 'build' command.
Error details:
java.lang.NullPointerException
  at java.base/java.util.Objects.requireNonNull(Objects.java:233)
  at java.base/java.util.ImmutableCollections$List12.<init>(ImmutableCollections.java:563)
  at java.base/java.util.List.of(List.java:937)
  at io.quarkus.paths.OpenContainerPathTree.getRoots(OpenContainerPathTree.java:96)

  at io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.PathTreeClassPathElement.toString(PathTreeClassPathElement.java:214)
  at java.base/java.util.Formatter$FormatSpecifier.printString(Formatter.java:3158)
  at java.base/java.util.Formatter$FormatSpecifier.print(Formatter.java:3036) 
```

The root cause is not clear.
Stacktrace of root cause is suppressed.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Add any extension, with a dependency to another jar, to `providers` directory
2. Exclude the dependency from `providers` directory
3. Run Keycloak building as `kc.bat|sh build --verbose`

### Anything else?

Existing issue with same error
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/35742

issue_title: Duplicated code due to typo in DefaultHttpClientFactory
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

_No response_

### Describe the bug

Commit https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/c3a15cb3 introduced support to get the reuse connections config property from a sys env.
However, the configuration value was assigned to a variable with a minor spelling error.

The new variable was used to set the `reuseConnections` property of the `HttpClientBuilder`, but the property is also set a few lines up in the same code block.

This should be cleaned up to avoid later errors or confusion.

Related code:
```java
private void lazyInit(KeycloakSession session) {
    ...
    boolean reuseConnections = config.getBoolean("reuse-connections", true);
    ...
    boolean resuseConnections = getBooleanConfigWithSysPropFallback("reuse-connections", true);
    ...
    HttpClientBuilder builder = newHttpClientBuilder();
    builder.socketTimeout(socketTimeout, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)
            ...
            .reuseConnections(reuseConnections)
            ...
            .reuseConnections(resuseConnections);
```

26.1.0

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No code cuplication within the same block

### Actual behavior

...

...

issue_title: Viewing user events requires `view-realm`-role
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

With 26.1.0 a new tab was added to the user details page in the admin console, allowing to view all events related to the open user.

To view this tab, a user has to have the role `view-realm`. Otherwise it just doesn't exist.
This seems to be a mistake. To view the entire events list, a user needs the role `view-events`. I think if a user has this role, he should also have this new tab.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A user in the admin console should see a events tab in the user details page if he has the role `view-events`.

### Actual behavior

A user can only see this tab if he has the role `view-realm`.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Get a Keycloak instance
2. Create a user and give him the role `view-events`
3. Log into the admin console as this user, open a user detail page and notice the events tab missing
4. Remove the `view-events` role of that user and replace it with the `view-realm` role.
5. Log into the admin console as this user, open a user detail page and notice the events tab being shown

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Don't require events enabled to view event tabs. (#36722)

* Don't require events enabled to view event tabs.

Fixes #36527

Signed-off-by: Stan Silvert <ssilvert@redhat.com>

* Fix test.

Fixes #36527

Signed-off-by: Stan Silvert <ssilvert@redhat.com>

issue_title: WebAuthN and dark mode: device icons are hardly readable
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication/webauthn

### Describe the bug

During login with dark mode enabled, when getting the list of configured WebAuthN devices, the icons are hardly readable: see screenshot.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5966caf2-35ed-4227-8eb4-6c200a97a68a)

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

During login with dark mode enabled, when getting the list of configured WebAuthN devices, the icons are easily readable: see screenshot.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9dcb5e62-593a-41d5-8854-862436f8349e)

### Actual behavior

During login with dark mode enabled, when getting the list of configured WebAuthN devices, the icons are hardly readable: see screenshot.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5966caf2-35ed-4227-8eb4-6c200a97a68a)

### How to Reproduce?

Configure WebAuthN devices for your account. It does not matter whether these are actual hardware tokens, Android phones, iPhones or any other passkey-enabled password manager.

Enable dark mode in your browser.

Log in to your account.

### Anything else?

_No response_

commit_message:
Keycloak can only be built with JDK 17 or JDK 21

As experienced in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/36541,
it fails to build in various ways if you try to
use JDK 23.

Also add instructions on how to specify JDK version when building
(helpful when you have multiple JDK versions installed).

Closes #36541

Signed-off-by: Eric Eastwood <contact@ericeastwood.com>

issue_title: keycloak.v2 forms are too small for mobile view
issue_body:
### Description

When using a phone and opening the login page of keycloak with the keycloak.v2 theme, it looks like this:
![grafik](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/413038dd-6e4a-453e-b83e-ac1e0ccf5984)

This is **way too small**. We should update the theme to be more mobile-friendly.

In comparison, the old `keycloak` theme login form wasn't great, but it was more usable:
![grafik](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/968f118f-e4b7-4ad6-98b5-353f4263691b)

### Discussion

#36555

_No response_

_No response_

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

In some scenarios, a resource may have been removed of the KC server but still exists in the path cache of the policy enforcer. Consequently, the policy enforcer cannot obtain a permission token from the KC server for these deleted resources and throw a runtime error.

### Version

26.0.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The policy enforcer should evict/update resources from the cache.

### Actual behavior

Permission request for deleted resources throw a runtime error from the policy enforcer

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure an service with authz + policy enforcer + cache enabled
2. Ask for permission to access to a protected resource and obtain a permission ticket
3. Remove the protected resource in server
4. Ask again for permission to the service to access to the deleted protected resource => 500 error

` Error creating permission ticket
2025-01-16T10:43:57 	at org.keycloak.authorization.client.util.Throwables.retryAndWrapExceptionIfNecessary(Throwables.java:91) ~[keycloak-authz-client-26.0.3.jar:26.0.3]
2025-01-16T10:43:57 	at org.keycloak.authorization.client.resource.PermissionResource.create(PermissionResource.java:121) ~[keycloak-authz-client-26.0.3.jar:26.0.3]
2025-01-16T10:43:57 	at org.keycloak.authorization.client.resource.PermissionResource.create(PermissionResource.java:96) ~[keycloak-authz-client-26.0.3.jar:26.0.3]
2025-01-16T10:43:57 	at org.keycloak.adapters.authorization.PolicyEnforcer.getPermissionTicket(PolicyEnforcer.java:564) ~[keycloak-policy-enforcer-26.0.3.jar:26.0.3]
2025-01-16T10:43:57 	at org.keycloak.adapters.authorization.PolicyEnforcer.challenge(PolicyEnforcer.java:337) ~[keycloak-policy-enforcer-26.0.3.jar:26.0.3]`

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Add new error type in authz client and handle properly missing resources errors to enable policy enforcer resource cache eviction/update.

Closes #36560

Signed-off-by: Emilien Bondu <dev.ebondu@gmail.com>

issue_title: Keycloak user attribute key broken in Keycloak 26.1.0
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/36307#issuecomment-2599759736

Following from this comment, I found a more serious problem due to the bug mentioned in the comment.

Because the keys associated with the profile change due to this bug, the user data from the token is lost because the key is wrong (for new registered users).

The first name and last name for the newly registered user are empty now.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/73ae2b26-267a-40e3-ab4c-cf16b8a6b730)

The problem is that when clicking `Save` from the `Realm settings -> User profile -> Edit attribute` page:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/16b1df9a-d98d-44e2-8456-c1754d7c8a97)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/117e9c80-ccd3-4caa-8516-18050674ba73)

The original attriute key will change automatically. The user attribute is changing from `lastName` to `profile.attributes.lastName`
 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d4097961-08f3-4e49-8b62-7f0289fc7e1a)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/79cbdc4f-fc48-4ff0-8a7c-37b98b25ff60)


But in the mappers, the original key is used.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cd6d99c2-d652-4773-a1ed-667162efe106)

same for `firstName`. So the `User's full name` is missing therefore. Causing 500 Internal Server Error from the backend when I try to read the user's full name.

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I expect that the user attribute will not change after clicking `Save`

### Actual behavior

The default attributes are changing if clicking `Save`.

### How to Reproduce?

Go to `Realm settings -> User profile -> Attribute [Name]`, click `Save`, see that the error toast message will pop-up and the attribute key (`Display name`) is changing!

### Anything else?

I was updating from Keycloak 24.0.5 to Keycloak 26.1.0.

_No response_

issue_title: Links error for https://jwt.io in documentation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

The links check in documentation are failing for `https://jwt.io`. For example in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/12903513066/job/35978901869

```
https://jwt.io/ (invalid status code 500)
```

The reason is that now jwt.io seems to require the `Accept-Language` header:

```bash
curl -s -o /dev/null -I -w "%{http_code}\n" https://jwt.io
500
curl -s -o /dev/null -I -w "%{http_code}\n" -H "Accept-Language: en" https://jwt.io
200
```

I'll add the workaround to send that header in the links class.

### Version

999

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No error

### Actual behavior

https://jwt.io/ (invalid status code 500)

### How to Reproduce?

```
cd docs/documentation/
mvn clean install
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

([ref](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/docs/guides/server/fips.adoc?plain=1#L34-L35))

The docs do not tell alternative facts

The docs tell alternative facts

issue_title: When linking IDP to an organization hide on login sets as off
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When linking IDP to an organization hide on login which defaults to "ON" automatically sets as "OFF" upon clicking save button. You cannot touch the toggle otherwise it sets correctly. 

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If the toggle defaults to ON and it is not changed, it should stay as ON after saving. 

### Actual behavior

The hide on login toggle defaults to ON and it is changed to OFF after saving (if you didn't turn it off and on before saving). 

### How to Reproduce?

1. create an org in the realm
2. create an IDP i.e Facebook with dummy client id and secret
3. try to link to the org with "hide on login=ON" without touching the toggle
4. after you linked the IDP, the hide on login=OFF in the table

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Make hideOnLogin default to true on submit. (#36805)

Fixes #36703

issue_title: After importing SAML client certificate the client is broken and can't be saved
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

After updating keycloak I was no longer able to import SAML PEM-certificates as trusted within the SAML client configuration

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The certificate is stored as trusted certificate to verify SAMLRequest Signatures

### Actual behavior

An Error message appears, stating:

Client could not be updated: Invalid json representation for ClientRepresentation. Unrecognized field "format" at line 1 column 4173.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cefaf2df-31b1-4de5-8d14-9ebe38f9a7e7)

### How to Reproduce?

1. Start fresh kc docker container from quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:26.1.0
2. Log in as Admin
3. Create new Realm
4. Create new Client in Realm
5. Client-Type: SAML
6. Go to "Keys" tab within that client
7. Click "Import Key" within the "Certificates" section
8. Choose "Certificate PEM" from the Dropdown
9. Click "Browse"
10. Choose self-signed certificate file
